dimensions.rubric: Distinct vocabulary and mostly distinct authority, with leaks.
dimensions.score_context: One confirmed context (Phase 2). Vocabulary mostly coherent (Phase 1); one same-term-different-meaning collision around 'user-agnostic' (mild) and one drift in 'WYN OPS' references (captured as F-001). Lesson-content portability collision around 'portable' captured as F-003.

dimensions.rubric: Domain purity and dependency direction are enforced.
dimensions.score_context: Vacuously satisfied: shelltutor is a single-file CLI tutor; there is no domain layer to leak into adapters, and no application orchestrator to leak business rules into UI. The shape of the codebase does not allow these violations. Score 3 reflects 'no leak observed because no structural plane exists to leak between.'

dimensions.rubric: Partial schema coverage; producer/consumer or validation unclear.
dimensions.score_context: Phase 4 inventoried six de-facto contracts (CLI args, exit codes, env vars, sandbox layout, usage text, lesson heredocs). All classified 'ad-hoc'. Producer/consumer is clear from code; validation exists for some inputs (read_progress regex, argv case statement) but nothing is schema-defined. F-007 records this. Appropriate for project scope; score 1 honestly applied per the rubric.

dimensions.rubric: Registry includes descriptions, schemas, protocol versions, authority scopes, approval policies, lifecycle modes, and version pins.
dimensions.score_context: Vacuously satisfied: there are no tools in the agent-runtime sense. The script's internal functions (next/prev/show/quit/exit) are bash navigation builtins overridden at shelltutor:126-130, well-named and non-overlapping. Score 3 reflects 'no surface to be unclear.'

dimensions.rubric: Stores and memory surfaces are classified with retention, owners, and delete/reset paths.
dimensions.score_context: Phase 5 inventoried five stores (S-001..S-004 + S-DIR). Each has owner, retention, write authority, deletion path. The narrower concern is the gap between A-002's declared filesystem scope ($SANDBOX) and actual subshell scope — captured as F-002.

dimensions.rubric: Per-tool/action/principal authority declared with approval gates for write paths, but bypass/precedence/callback/secondary-credential handling may have gaps.
dimensions.score_context: Three approval gates in AGENTS.md:43-78 (contributor-side); approval modes and precedence recorded. A-001 (script) honors its declared sandbox. A-002 (practice subshell) declared vs actual scope mismatch is F-002 (focus question). Enforcement of contributor-side gates is via norms (CONTRIBUTING.md:72-75), not code — this is intentional posture but lowers the score from 3 to 2.

dimensions.rubric: Logs only or no meaningful telemetry.
dimensions.score_context: No telemetry. Score 0 honestly applied. Context per Phase 7: this is appropriate for a Day-1 CLI tutor with no agentic runtime, no production deployment, no SLA. The score should not be read as 'observability expected and missing.'

dimensions.rubric: Runtime and server-exposed prompts are mostly parameterized from policy-controlled sources; authoring artifacts or privileged-context boundaries may drift.
dimensions.score_context: No runtime LLM prompts (vacuous). AGENTS.md (skill-or-sop) has six embedded-policy rules without code-level enforcement (P-001, F-004); contradictory-guidance drift between STATUS/ROADMAP and commit log (F-001). Score 2 reflects skill-or-sop class doing its job overall, with the safety-rule enforcement gap pulling away from 3.

dimensions.rubric: No eval coverage for model/agent/automation paths.
dimensions.score_context: No evals. Score 0 honestly applied. Context per Phase 9: no agentic runtime to eval; ROADMAP Phase 2 plans manual walkthrough + shellcheck (pending). F-006 captures the gap.

dimensions.rubric: Governance artifacts are current enough to guide the audit; some drift remains.
dimensions.score_context: Comprehensive governance docs (AGENTS, CLAUDE, CONTRIBUTING, STATUS, ROADMAP, README). Decision-Disagreement Rule (STATUS.md:65-69) and conflict-resolution rule (CLAUDE.md:14-22, AGENTS.md:29-30) are explicit. Drift in STATUS/ROADMAP vs commit log (F-001). Absolute-path citation in AGENTS.md:22 (F-005). No ADRs (no ADR template required by current ROADMAP). Score 2.
No fitness functions at the snapshot revision. The seven candidates in 10-fitness-functions.{md,json} are proposals; none are implemented yet.

aggregate_summary.lowest_dimensions_note: All three score 0 because the underlying capability is absent and intentionally so for a Day-1 CLI tutor with no agentic runtime. The score should be read with context, not as a bare 'low.'
